requirements: Non-functional educational engine_display cartridge
Combustion_chamber_dummy, injector-face visual insert, cutaway_window, slide_rail
No pressure/ignition/flight capability
Removable engine display cartridge: combustion chamber dummy, bell nozzle shell, turbopump dummy in cutaway bay
Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating
Tank, interstage, avionics, recovery, and engine-display modules are labeled educational/display-only
Cutaway shows tank dummies, interstage datum, avionics bay, recovery bay, and removable engine display cartridge
Risk/verification focuses on mockup structure, inspection, interfaces, and unsafe overclaiming
Interstage and module joints show locating datums, bolt-circle dummies, and alignment keys so the mock stack assembles repeatably for review

risk.desc: Potential structural weakness at the combustion chamber dummy mount.
risk.mit: Increase material thickness or use a stronger alloy.

risk.mit: Implement thermal simulation and adjust materials accordingly.

risk.mit: Ensure all access points are clearly marked and easily accessible.

risk.mit: Use high-precision manufacturing techniques for alignment keys.
